title: Detect complex multi-step requests using morphological verb count
goal: kiwipiepy 형태소 분석으로 명령어의 동사(VV, VX, XSV) 개수를 세서 복합 요청을 감지한다. 동사 2개 이상이면 AI 멀티스텝 계획으로 라우팅하고, 1개 이하면 기존 규칙 매칭을 사용한다. kiwipiepy 미설치 시 연결어미 패턴 폴백.

test_requirements.case: Simple crawl request uses rule match
Complex request with 3 verbs routes to AI
Fallback without kiwipiepy still detects complex via connectives
test_requirements.expected: 크롤링하고 요약해서 → is_complex=True even without kiwipiepy

constraints: complexity.py provides: count_action_verbs(command) -> int and is_complex_request(command) -> bool
Use kiwipiepy morphological analysis: count tokens with POS tags VV, VX, XSV (Korean verb tags)
If kiwipiepy not installed, fall back to counting Korean connective endings (고, 서, 면, 후) between clauses
In router.py: if is_complex_request(command) and has_api_key(), skip rule match and go to AI plan
Do not modify normalizer.py or plugins
Do not break existing tests for simple single-action commands
Import from intent.korean module where kiwipiepy usage already exists
test_command: cd backend && python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v
